results.input: AgriSense AI is an agri-tech startup using AI and IoT. It is in early growth stage after MVP validation.

expected.startup_overview: AgriSense AI is an agri-tech startup that leverages Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) to provide data-driven insights and automation for modern farming. By integrating sensor data with predictive analytics, the company helps farmers increase crop yields and reduce resource waste.
expected.problem_statement: The agricultural sector faces significant inefficiencies due to unpredictable weather, sub-optimal resource allocation, and a lack of real-time data on soil and crop health, leading to lower yields and higher costs.
expected.solution_summary: AgriSense AI provides an end-to-end platform combining IoT hardware and AI software to monitor field conditions and automate irrigation and fertilization, optimizing productivity through precise diagnostics.
expected.target_customers: Commercial farmers, agricultural cooperatives, and large-scale plantation owners seeking operational efficiency.
expected.business_model: Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) model with subscription tiers for the monitoring platform, plus revenue from IoT hardware sales.

results.input: MediLink is a healthcare startup providing telemedicine solutions and is scaling beyond prototype stage. They use AI-driven diagnostics.

expected.startup_overview: MediLink is a healthcare technology startup providing an advanced telemedicine platform that integrates AI-driven diagnostics. The platform connects patients with specialists remotely and assists doctors with automated symptom analysis and diagnostic data visualization.
expected.problem_statement: Limited access to specialized medical care in rural and underserved urban areas, combined with diagnostic delays, results in poor health outcomes and increased mortality.
expected.solution_summary: A secure telemedicine platform that uses AI algorithms to analyze diagnostic data and provide preliminary assessments, facilitating faster and more accurate remote medical interventions.
expected.target_customers: Patients in remote areas, private clinics, and hospital networks looking to scale their digital health offerings.
expected.business_model: Transactional model based on consultation fees and B2B licensing for clinics and hospitals.

results.input: Bharat Fintech Solutions is a fintech startup enabling UPI-based micro-lending for rural India. Early revenue stage with 5,000+ users.

expected.startup_overview: Bharat Fintech Solutions is a financial services startup focused on rural financial inclusion through UPI-based micro-lending. The platform uses alternative credit scoring and UPI integration to provide small, low-interest loans to underserved populations.
expected.problem_statement: The lack of formal credit history and physical banking infrastructure prevents millions in rural India from accessing emergency funds or capital for micro-enterprises.
expected.solution_summary: A mobile-first lending platform that leverages UPI rails and alternative data points (like utility payments) to assess creditworthiness and disburse micro-loans instantly.
expected.target_customers: Rural micro-entrepreneurs, small-scale farmers, and daily wage earners in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
expected.business_model: Interest-based revenue model on disbursed loans, with processing fees and data-driven credit assessment services for larger financial partners.

Key tracks include Digital & FinTech Inclusion, Agri-Tech, and Healthcare. Selected startups receive grants up to ₹5 Lakh, expert mentorship, and incubation support. Open to startups at all stages from idea to early revenue.

results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...FutureX AI – Campus Fund (2nd Edition) by Campus Fund, Google CloudCampus Fund, Google CloudAmountUpto ₹8.5 CrDeadlineApr 10, 2026TypeacceleratorCategoriesTechnologyTagsaistudent-leddeeptech+6 moreAbout this OpportunityFutureX AI is the second edition of Campus Fund's student startup platform, run with Google Cloud. It provides India's biggest platform for student-led AI startups. Selected teams receive investment up to $1,000,000 from Campus Fund and Google Cloud credits through the Google for Startups Cloud Program. The program includes workshops on AI product building, fundraising, and scaling. Participants also attend masterclasses focused on AI innovations. A Grand Finale event will be held at the Google Bengaluru office on May 29, 2026, followed by long-term post-program support for selected startups. Applications are open to current students (undergraduate, postgraduate, or research) or graduates/dropouts from 2023 onwards. Applicants must hold at least 25% equity in their AI-first startup. The program seeks startups across foundational models, AI in healthcare, climate, fintech, agritech, and AI-enabled hardware.Eligibility The startup must be led by founders who are currently enrolled as students (undergraduate, postgraduate, or research) at any university, or by individuals who have graduated or dropped out from a university program in 2023 or any year thereafter. Founders leading the startup must collectively possess a minimum equity stake of 25% in their venture. The startup's core operations must be firmly rooted in the artificial intelligence domain, explicitly demonstrating the application of Machine Learning, Generative AI, or advanced Data Intelligence technologies in its products or services. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...BioVerse Challenge 2025 by Thermo Fisher ScientificThermo Fisher ScientificAmountNot specifiedDeadlineApr 10, 2026TypeacceleratorStageearly_revenuegrowthCategoriesTechnologyTagsbiotechnologyhealthcarelife-sciences+5 moreAbout this OpportunityThermo Fisher Scientific, a global leader in serving science, has launched the BioVerse Challenge 2025, a nationwide initiative designed to accelerate high-potential biotech startups across India. This program aims to foster world-leading innovation within India's bioeconomy by providing critical resources for scaling and commercialization. The challenge targets innovations across several key areas, including Biologics, Industrial Biotechnology, Bioenergy, Green Biotech, Marine Biotechnology, Agri & Food Biotech, and Medtech & Diagnostics. The challenge categorizes participants into two distinct groups: Trailblazers and Rising Stars. Trailblazers, a maximum of 20 startups, receive comprehensive support including access to Thermo Fisher's state-of-the-art facilities and technologies in India, customized skill-building programs, and expert mentorship. They also gain access to strategic partnerships and collaboration opportunities. Rising Stars, comprising up to 50 startups, specifically benefit from expert mentorship, strategic partnerships, and collaboration opportunities, focusing on advancing their ventures through targeted guidance. The program underscores a commitment to enhancing India's biotech ecosystem.Eligibility The program specifically supports startups founded by Indian nationals who are ly seeking access to cutting-edge technology and expert mentorship to advance their innovations. Applicants must be formally registered with DPIIT/Startup India to be considered for this challenge. The BioVerse Challenge is designed for early-stage and growth-stage startups that exhibit significant potential for successful scale-up and commercialization within the biotech sector. Participating startups must demonstrate clear technology prowess, ensuring their solutions are well-aligned with existing market needs and demands. A defined or evolving business model is required, alongside a clear and compelling value proposition that outlines the startup's unique offering and competitive advantage. Linkage with incubators, academic institutions, government bodies, or industry partners is considered an added advantage during evaluation. Application Process Startups must submit a detailed business case covering problem, solution, market, model, financials, traction, impact, and support needed. Evaluation by Thermo Fisher Scientific and a BioVerse Advisory Board of experts from industry, government, and academia. Selected startups are designated as Trailblazers or Rising Stars based on this evaluation. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...R|Elan Circular Design Challenge 2026R|Elan (Reliance Industries), United Nations IndiaAmount₹5 Lakhs – ₹15 LakhsDeadlineApr 14, 2026TypecontestStageideamvpearly_revenueCategoriesEnvironmentTagscircular-fashionsustainable-textilesbiomaterials+6 moreAbout this OpportunityR|Elan Circular Design Challenge is a global design competition by R|Elan, an initiative of Reliance Industries, in partnership with United Nations India, Lakmé Fashion Week, and FDCI. It aims to identify and support designers, entrepreneurs, and startups engaged in sustainable fashion innovation. The program offers significant support including seed funding and mentorship from global sustainability experts. Participants are selected to showcase their collections at Lakmé Fashion Week x FDCI in India. The total prize pool exceeds Rs. 20 lakhs, distributed between a winner and a runner-up. The winner receives INR 15 lakhs in seed funding, a trophy, and six months of mentorship. The runner-up is awarded INR 5 lakhs in seed funding and mentorship. The challenge focuses on promoting circularity across the textile value chain through the use of recycled materials, innovative dyes, resource efficiency, and waste reduction. It seeks original, unpublished work demonstrating measurable environmental and social benefits within the sustainable fashion and textile industry.Eligibility Organizations or labels are eligible, provided they have been operating for a maximum of eight years. Individual designers are encouraged to apply, as are consortiums composed of up to four professionals. The competition is open to global applicants, with assessments conducted regionally across EU, UK, India, Asia Pacific, and the rest of the world. Startups and entrepreneurs ly developing innovations within the sustainable fashion sector are specifically targeted. All submissions are mandated to explicitly demonstrate principles of circularity throughout the entire textile value chain. Eligible projects must incorporate and showcase the use of recycled materials, innovative alternative dyes, strategies for resource efficiency, and/or proven methods for waste reduction. Submissions are required to clearly articulate and evidence measurable environmental benefits and positive social impacts. Only original work that has not been previously published or showcased will be considered for the challenge. Application Process Applications are reviewed by an expert panel consisting of designers, sustainability organizations, media, and UN representatives. Shortlisted participants are required to attend in-person jury events in their respective countries. Finalists must be physically present for model fittings and the final showcase at Lakmé Fashion Week x FDCI in India. The program provides deep, expert-led support to help entrepreneurs grow their ventures effectively through structured capacity building, curated resources, high-quality mentoring, and strong networking opportunities. Participants gain access to strategic guidance from Amazon leaders and industry experts, frameworks for scaling operations, exclusive learning tracks, and brand-building support for becoming successful D2C businesses. The program also provides visibility on Amazon Saheli storefronts, opportunities to participate in Amazon campaigns, and recognition through Saheli certification. A key highlight is the direct access to venture capitalists during Demo Day, where selected founders can pitch for funding support. The program supports women-led enterprises working across varied sectors, including sustainability, social impact, and e-commerce–focused product innovation.Eligibility Women-led or women-founded businesses, cooperatives, or self-help groups. Businesses registered in India as a sole proprietorship, partnership, LLP, or private limited company. Entrepreneurs who are sellers or planning to launch their business on Amazon.in. Applicants must demonstrate potential for growth, product innovation, and readiness to participate in accelerator activities. Application Process Apply through the link given below. Submit the required business and founder details along with supporting documents. Shortlisted applicants will be evaluated based on product innovation, business potential, and alignment with the program objectives. Selected founders will be onboarded into the accelerator cohort and provided with mentorship, growth resources, and program benefits. The program is designed to accelerate early-stage, technology-driven AgTech startups that address critical challenges related to global food security and sustainability. Unveiled at the 2024 Norman E. Borlaug International Dialogue, the Challenge invites entrepreneurs from around the world to showcase breakthrough agricultural technologies with the potential to transform food systems. Key Highlights Global call for early-stage AgTech startups Strong emphasis on innovation, sustainability, and scalability Live pitch competition at the 2026 Borlaug Dialogue in Des Moines, Iowa Top prize of $50,000 USD for the winning startup Significant global exposure to investors, policymakers, and agricultural leaders Eligibility Must be an early-stage, for-profit startup operating in the AgTech space Stage of development can range from validated concept to pre-Series A At least one founder must be working full-time on the startup Must demonstrate: Strong innovation and technological differentiation Clear market potential within the agricultural sector Alignment with environmental and social sustainability goals Applications are evaluated based on: Innovation impact and novelty Market viability and demand Sustainability contribution Scalability and replicability across regions and contexts Application Process Create an account on the application portal and start a new application. Complete the full online application form with all required information. Submit the application before the deadline. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...BioNEST Incubation Program – FITT IIT DelhiFITT IIT Delhi, BIRACAmountNot specifiedDeadlineApr 15, 2026TypeincubationStageideamvpearly_revenueCategoriesHealthcareTechnologyTagsbiotechnologybiomedical devicesnanotechnology+7 moreAbout this OpportunityThe BioNEST Incubation Program, operated by FITT (Foundation for Innovation and Technology Transfer) at IIT Delhi and generously supported by BIRAC (Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council), this program is a dedicated biotechnology business incubation facility under BIRAC's BISS (Bioincubators Igniting Novel and Emerging Technologies in Science) initiative, underscoring its central role in the national biotech ecosystem. Its primary purpose is to provide a nurturing and resource-rich environment for biotech innovators and startups, particularly those at the ideation, proof-of-concept, or early prototype stages, transforming novel scientific ideas into viable market-ready products. The program offers crucial in-kind support, including access to shared laboratory equipment and cutting-edge facilities located within the esteemed IIT Delhi campus, minimizing the prohibitive costs typically associated with biotech R&D. Beyond physical infrastructure, incubatees benefit from comprehensive mentoring by renowned IIT Delhi faculty and seasoned industry experts, guiding them through scientific and business challenges. The ecosystem support extends to critical business development areas such as business plan refinement, intellectual property rights (IPR) filing guidance, and technology transfer assistance, ensuring that innovations are protected and commercialized effectively. The program also facilitates extensive networking opportunities with investors and corporate partners, essential for future growth and market penetration. With a remarkable track record of incubating 38 innovators and startups, leading to 24 market-launched products, over 30 intellectual property filings, and the creation of more than 200 jobs, alongside raising over Rs 50 crore in funds, the BioNEST Incubation Program at FITT IIT Delhi significantly contributes to India's biotech landscape, fostering first-generation entrepreneurs and driving innovation in biotechnology, biomedical devices, and nanotechnology.Eligibility Biotech innovators and startups are eligible. Startups must be at ideation, proof-of-concept, or early prototype stages. Focus sectors include Biotechnology, biomedical devices, and nanotechnology. First-generation entrepreneurs in biotech are specifically targeted. IIT Delhi students and faculty are preferred, but not exclusively. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...Cohort 7 Incubation Program at VJTI TBI FoundationVJTI TBI FoundationAmountNot specifiedDeadlineApr 17, 2026TypeincubationCategoriesTechnologyTagsincubationenergyelectric vehicles+6 moreAbout this OpportunityThe VJTI Technology Business Incubator (TBI) Foundation, hosted at Veermata Jijabai Technological Institute in Mumbai, is launching Cohort 7 of its incubation program. VJTI TBI is a government-affiliated incubator focused on deep technology sectors. The program invites startups building in energy, electric vehicles, semiconductors, IoT, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cybersecurity to apply for a structured incubation experience. Selected startups receive access to state-of-the-art laboratory and testing infrastructure, which is central to product development and technical validation. The program provides entrepreneurship training and mentorship from experienced industry professionals, along with facilitated investor connections and networking to support fundraising. Participants also receive marketing and go-to-market support, assistance with talent recruitment, and advisory services covering business strategy and financial planning. Applications are open from March 28 to April 17, 2026. The program's core value is access to infrastructure, networks, and advisory support from VJTI TBI's ecosystem.Eligibility Startups must be focused on building products or solutions in at least one of the designated technology domains: Energy, Electric Vehicles (EV), Semiconductors, Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML), or Cyber Security. Applications must be submitted through the Startup India portal within the application window: March 28, 2026 to April 17, 2026. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...Google for Startups Accelerator: IndiaGoogle for StartupsAmountNot specifiedDeadlineApr 19, 2026TypeacceleratorStageearly_revenuegrowthCategoriesTechnologyTagsaimldeeptech+6 moreAbout this OpportunityThe Google for Startups Accelerator: India is a three-month, equity-free program designed for AI-first startups operating between the Seed and Series A stages. Google for Startups runs this accelerator to support innovative companies headquartered in India that are developing core AI applications or agentic AI solutions. The program offers equity-free support, dedicated mentoring from Google teams, and exclusive invitations to technical bootcamps. Participants receive access to Google's extensive network of industry experts, early access to new Google AI products via Trusted Tester and EAP benefits, and Google product credits. Eligible startups also benefit from Cloud credits and free Cloud TPU access for open-source machine learning research. The accelerator provides strategic support on company and product strategy, fostering technical project partnerships to address significant technological challenges. Each cohort, comprising 10-15 startups, addresses technical hurdles through a blend of remote and in-person sessions. The curriculum includes one-on-one and group learning, alongside sprint projects. Founders collaborate with Google experts and industry specialists to outline and resolve their primary technical challenges. Deep dives and workshops also cover critical areas like product design, customer acquisition, and leadership development.Eligibility Startups must be demonstrating clear market traction, ideally positioning them between the Seed and Series A funding stages. The applicant startup is required to be based or headquartered within India. Applicants need to be developing a scalable product or service designed to address a significant total addressable market. The proposed product or service must also exhibit a strong and defensible growth model for sustained expansion. The startup's core operations must be deeply technical, extensively leveraging machine learning and advanced AI technologies. Eligibility extends to startups building core AI products, with a particular interest in agentic, multimodal, thinking/reasoning, or other innovative Generative AI applications. Startups focused on developing foundational models are also considered within the program's scope. A clear requirement is the identification of potential Google AI use cases integrated within the startup's technology or product offering. Founder(s) and other key personnel are expected to provide a firm commitment to ly participate in all mandatory program sessions. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...iAccelerate Batch 2Tamil Nadu Technology Hub (iTNT Hub)AmountUpto ₹40 LakhsDeadlineApr 20, 2026TypeacceleratorStagemvpearly_revenuegrowthCategoriesDeeptechTagsdeeptechacceleratortamil-nadu+3 moreAbout this OpportunityiAccelerate Batch 2 is an accelerator program spearheaded by the Tamil Nadu Technology Hub (iTNT Hub), an initiative of the Government of Tamil Nadu. This program is specifically designed to support deep tech and emerging technology startups originating from Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities within Tamil Nadu. It targets ventures that have progressed beyond the ideation stage, demonstrating readiness for market entry and growth. The program focuses on startups that have achieved TRL 3 and above, evidenced by a working prototype, a Minimum Viable Product (MVP), or early market traction. iAccelerate provides expert-led strategy sessions, offering crucial guidance for scaling. Participants gain access to corporate and government partners, connect with over 30 investor partners, and receive pitch opportunities to sector-focused investors, with seed funding of up to Rs. 40 lakh available for graduating startups.Eligibility The program is exclusively open to Deep Tech and Emerging Technology startups with founders or operations based in Tier 2 or Tier 3 cities in Tamil Nadu; startups from Chennai or other Tier 1 locations are not eligible. Applicants must be at Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 3 or above, meaning a working prototype, MVP, or early market traction is required; pure ideation-stage ventures are not considered. The program targets technology-first startups in areas such as AI, hardware, clean tech, health tech, agri tech, and advanced manufacturing — the solution must be built around a deep or emerging technology. Startups must be ly operating beyond the concept stage with a clearly defined product or technology already in development or early deployment. Graduating startups from the cohort are eligible to apply for seed funding of up to Rs. 40 lakh each, subject to evaluation criteria set by iTNT Hub. Applicants must be willing to participate in expert-led strategy sessions, investor pitches, and structured mentoring engagements throughout the program duration. results.input: 🎉 95% off on Lovable → Exclusive dealLoading...FITT IIT Delhi – BIRAC LEAP Fund for Biotech & MedTech StartupsFITT, IIT DelhiAmountUpto ₹70 LakhsDeadlineApr 20, 2026TypefundingStagemvpearly_revenueCategoriesHealthcareTagsbiracbiotechmedtech+7 moreAbout this OpportunityThe LEAP Fund is a flagship funding initiative supported by BIRAC and administered through the Foundation for Innovation and Technology Transfer (FITT) at IIT Delhi. It is designed to support high-potential startups operating in the biotechnology and healthcare sectors that have progressed beyond early-stage validation and are preparing for pilot deployment or early commercialization. The program specifically focuses on startups building innovative, IP-driven solutions that address critical unmet needs in healthcare and life sciences. The fund provides equity-linked investment of up to ₹70 lakh, acting as a crucial bridge between proof-of-concept and market entry. This capital support enables startups to refine their products, conduct validation studies, navigate regulatory pathways, and accelerate commercialization timelines. The structure of the funding ensures alignment with long-term growth, as it is tied to the startup's equity and future potential rather than being a non-dilutive grant. In addition to financial support, selected startups gain access to IIT Delhi's deep innovation ecosystem. This includes co-working spaces and advanced laboratory infrastructure, along with mentorship from experienced academicians, clinicians, and industry experts. Startups also receive guidance on regulatory compliance, clinical validation, intellectual property strategy, and business model development, all of which are critical for healthcare ventures.Eligibility Startups must operate in biotechnology, healthcare, diagnostics, biopharma, bioprinting, personalized medicine, or tissue engineering sectors Must be developing novel, IP-driven products; service-based or pure IT businesses are not eligible More than 51% of shareholding must be held by resident Indian individuals Must be registered as a Private Limited Company Startups that have previously received LEAP funding from any other incubator are not eligible Application Process Submit application through FITT's official online portal Initial screening to verify eligibility and assess innovation relevance Detailed evaluation of the startup's technology, IP strength, market potential, and commercialization readiness Assessment of founding team's technical expertise and business capabilities Shortlisted applicants may be invited to present their solution through a pitch or demo session Final selection is made based on overall viability and strategic fit, followed by equity-linked funding offer and optional onboarding into FITT incubation programs Apply OnlineSharestartupgrantsindia.com/fitt-iit-delhi-birac-leap-fund-biotech-medtech-startups-456CopyWhatsAppLinkedInRecommended ProgramsIHFC–India Accelerator MedTech Impact Call 2026IHFC, IIT Delhi & India AcceleratorUp to ₹5 CrHealthcare Incubation Program – NSRCEL, IIM BangaloreNSRCEL, IIM BangaloreBioNEST Incubation Program – FITT IIT DelhiFITT IIT Delhi, BIRACTamil Nadu SC/ST Startup Fund (Equity Fund) by StartupTNStartupTNC-CAMP Wadhwani Innovation Network (WIN) Centre of Excellence Non-Dilutive Funding ProgramCentre for Cellular and Molecular Platforms (C-CAMP), Wadhwani FoundationUp to ₹1 CrPopular DealsLovable₹4L+ Worth Lovable AI Credits at 98% discount!NotionUnlock Rs.6000+ worth of NOTION Credits for FREEZohoUnlock Rs.9000+ worth of ZOHO Credits for FREE
